---
title: "Research"
description: "CompOmics research themes: peptide and protein identification, AI-based spectrum and retention-time prediction, and FAIR proteomics data."
---

## Overview

CompOmics is a bioinformatics research group at [Ghent University](https://www.ugent.be) and [VIB](https://vib.be), focused on computational proteomics. We develop open-source software and methods to push the boundaries of mass spectrometry-based proteomics.

---

## Research Themes

### Peptide & Protein Identification

Development of database search engines, post-processing tools, and validation strategies for PSM-level and protein-level identification in DDA and DIA workflows.

### AI-Based Spectrum & Retention Time Prediction

We train and apply deep learning models to predict MS2 spectra, retention times, and other peptide properties, enabling data-independent analysis and rescoring workflows.

### FAIR Proteomics Data

We contribute to community standards including HUPO-PSI formats (mzML, mzTab, mzSpecLib), ProteomeXchange/PRIDE data sharing, and ProteomicsML for machine learning-ready datasets.

### Proteomics Data Infrastructure

Building scalable data formats (Parquet, Feather), APIs, and pipelines to make large-scale proteomics data accessible and reusable by the community.

---

## Collaborations & Consortia

- [HUPO-PSI](https://psidev.info/)
- [ProteomeXchange](http://www.proteomexchange.org/)
- [PRIDE](https://www.ebi.ac.uk/pride/)
- [ProteomicsML](https://www.proteomicsml.org/)
